s2.q: What is the incidence of breech presentation at term?
s2.explain: Breech presentation occurs in 3-4% of all deliveries. It is the most common malpresentation at term.

s2.q: Which of the following is a key concept?
options: It is essential for understanding the condition
It is rarely relevant in clinical practice
It applies only to specific populations
It has been replaced by newer classifications
s2.explain: Understanding key concepts is fundamental to clinical management.

s2.explain: Assessment requires integration of clinical, laboratory, and imaging findings.

An incidental finding with no clinical significance
s2.explain: A definition describes the essential nature of a condition as a deviation from normal.

options: It applies only to research settings
s2.explain: Understanding these concepts directly informs clinical management.

s3.q: Which of the following is a predisposing factor for breech presentation?
s3.explain: Predisposing factors include: prematurity, uterine malformations/fibroids, polyhydramnios, placenta previa, fetal abnormalities, multiple gestations, cord around the neck, and pelvic tumors.

s3.q: The accommodation theory for breech presentation states that:
options: The fetus adapts to the pyriform shape of the uterus with buttocks in fundus and head in lower segment
Breech is caused by uterine contractions
The fetal head is always larger than the pelvis
s3.explain: The accommodation theory states that the fetus is usually adapted to the pyriform shape of the uterus with the large buttock in the fundus and the smaller head in the lower uterine segment.

s3.q: The etiology of this condition involves:
options: A complex interplay of genetic, environmental, and multifactorial elements
s3.explain: Most obstetric conditions have multifactorial etiology.

s3.q: Which of the following is a known risk factor?
options: All of the above may contribute depending on the condition
s3.explain: Multiple risk factors often contribute to obstetric conditions.

s3.q: In obstetric practice, this knowledge helps:
s3.explain: Comprehensive knowledge enables prevention, early diagnosis, and optimal management.

s4.q: Which type of breech is most common?
s4.explain: Frank breech is the most common type of breech presentation.

s5.q: What is the denominator in breech presentation?
s5.explain: In breech presentation, the sacrum is the denominator (reference point) for describing the position.

s6.q: On abdominal palpation of a breech presentation, the head is felt:
options: At the level of the umbilicus
s6.explain: In breech presentation, the head is felt in the fundus (the upper part of the uterus), while the buttocks are at the pelvic inlet.

s8.q: In the mechanism of labor for breech presentation, which part delivers first?
s8.explain: In breech labor, the breech (buttocks) delivers first, followed by the trunk, shoulders, and finally the head.

s9.q: Which maneuver is used to deliver the aftercoming head in breech delivery?
s9.explain: The Mauriceau-Smellie-Veit (MSV) maneuver is a method to deliver the aftercoming head in breech delivery.

s10.q: External Cephalic Version (ECV) is best performed at what gestational age?
s10.explain: ECV is typically performed at 36-37 weeks gestation to convert a breech to a cephalic presentation.

s11.q: Which of the following is a contraindication to vaginal breech delivery?
s11.explain: Footling breech is generally a contraindication to vaginal breech delivery due to risk of cord prolapse and entrapment.

After delivery of the trunk and arms
After full delivery of the fetus

s16.q: Which of the following is a complication of vaginal breech delivery?
s16.explain: Complications of vaginal breech delivery include cord prolapse, head entrapment, nuchal arm, and birth injuries.

s18.q: Which of the following is an indication for cesarean section in breech presentation?
options: Frank breech in a multiparous woman
s18.explain: Indications for CS in breech include estimated fetal weight > 3800g (macrosomia), hyperextended head, footling breech, and inadequate pelvis.

s19.q: Complicated breech delivery includes all EXCEPT:
s19.explain: Frank breech with a flexed head and adequate pelvis may be suitable for vaginal delivery. Complicated breech includes head entrapment, nuchal arm, and cord prolapse.
